prerequisites: A running Typesense server (self-hosted or Typesense Cloud) reachable at the host, port, and protocol you will configure.
A Typesense API key with sufficient permissions to write data, created via the /keys API using the server's bootstrap admin key (set with the --api-key server flag).
The host (hostname or IP) of your Typesense node for the required `host` config field.
Network access from the connector to the Typesense API endpoint (default port 8108 for self-hosted).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate against the Typesense server using an API key supplied in the `api_key` config field. Typesense sends this key in the X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY HTTP header on every request. Create a key by POSTing to the /keys endpoint using the bootstrap admin key configured with the server's --api-key flag, scoping it to the actions and collections the destination needs to write.

setupSteps.title: Provision the Typesense server and admin key
setupSteps.body: Stand up a Typesense node (self-hosted or Typesense Cloud). Self-hosted servers require a bootstrap admin API key passed via the --api-key startup flag, which grants all operations. Do not use this bootstrap key directly in production clients.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key for the connector
setupSteps.body: Using the bootstrap admin key, send a POST request to ${TYPESENSE_HOST}/keys to generate a key. Scope it with the `actions` (for example documents:create) and `collections` it is allowed to operate on. Typesense auto-generates the key value, which you supply to the connector via the `api_key` field.

setupSteps.title: Set the connection fields
setupSteps.body: Provide the required `host` (Typesense hostname or IP) and `api_key`. Optionally set `port` (default 8108 for self-hosted), `protocol` (http or https; Typesense supports HTTPS when SSL certificates are configured), and `path` if your Typesense API is served under a URL path prefix.

setupSteps.title: Tune batching (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `batch_size` to control how many records are sent per import request to Typesense. Leave it unset to use the connector default.
